string.text: Pocket personal computer. An indispensable device in the Zone.
Case with documents. Made from fire-resistant material.
Plastic file with documents. Without a biology degree, it's hard to make sense of them.
Thick stack of scientific papers. Full of terms, formulas, and diagrams.
Worn manuscript in a leather cover. Written by Strelok himself. Judging by the titles, it reveals some of the Zone's mysteries.
Heavy wooden crate marked "Explosive" in several languages.
Flash drive – a convenient device for data transfer.
Flash drive – a convenient device for data transfer.
Flash drive – a convenient device for data transfer.
Flash drive – a convenient device for data transfer.
Electronic key to underground lab X-18.
Pocket personal computer. An indispensable device in the Zone.
Pocket personal computer. An indispensable device in the Zone.
Case with documents. Made from fire-resistant material.
A sealed case made of fireproof alloy. Its contents seem to be classified.
Thick stack of scientific papers. Full of terms, formulas, and diagrams.
Plastic file with documents. Without a biology degree, it's hard to make sense of them.
A bundle of notes retrieved from Lab X10. Much of it is damaged or redacted, but key terms like "emitter" and "controller" repeat throughout.
Scientific documentation from Lab X8. Includes experiment logs, anomaly research, and some unsettling diagrams.
A weathered notebook recovered from Jupiter’s underground. Mentions odd seismic activity and something called "Project Urobros".
A sealed bundle of reports related to Monolith operations. The documents hint at coordination with unknown forces.
A personal notebook belonging to Strelok. Its pages are filled with sketches, encrypted entries, and reflections on the Zone's anomalies and secrets.
A collection of classified files recovered from Laboratory X8. They contain research data, experiment logs, and references to secret projects carried out in the depths of the facility.
A worn copybook filled with handwritten observations and fragmented formulas. Its content appears to be part of an abandoned research effort in Lab X8.
The second volume of a scientist’s personal notes. More detailed and chaotic, it hints at experiments that were never meant to be documented.
This is an ITEM test string
This is another ITEM test string

Powerful electronic lockpick. Strangely enough, it does not seem to be an all-purpose thieves' instrument. Rather, it seems to have been created with a specific electronic lock in mind and can't be used to override anything else.
Plastic document file. You'd need a degree in biology to understand a word of what it says.
A thick pile of scientific documents. Terms, formulas, and a number of diagrams.
Worn manuscript in leather binding. Written by Strelok himself. Judging by the headings alone, it might explain some of the Zone's secrets.
Documents from the secret stash in Pripyat.
A heavy wooden crate with the word "Explosives" written in several languages.
Flash drive - convenient way to store and transfer information.
Flash drive - convenient way to store and transfer information.
Flash drive - convenient way to store and transfer information.
Flash drive - convenient way to store and transfer information.
Electronic key to the underground Lab X18.
Personal digital assistant. Indispensable in the Zone.
Personal digital assistant. Indispensable in the Zone.
Prototype developed by scientists to protect people from psi-emissions. Not adjusted.
Functional prototype developed by scientists to protect people from psi-emissions.
